metadata.name: GPT-5.3-Codex
metadata.tooltip: OpenAI Codex model specialized for code generation, debugging, and software development tasks. Rate is counted at 1x.

agent.description: Edit files in your workspace in agent mode
agent.name: agent

slashCommands.name: compact
slashCommands.description: Free up context by compacting the conversation history. Optionally include extra instructions for compaction.

slashCommands.name: explain
slashCommands.description: Explain how the code in your active editor works

slashCommands.name: tests
slashCommands.description: Generate unit tests for the selected code

disambiguation.description: The user wants to generate unit tests.
examples: Generate tests for my selection using pytest.

slashCommands.name: fix
slashCommands.description: Propose a fix for the problems in the selected code
slashCommands.sampleRequest: There is a problem in this code. Rewrite the code to show it with the bug fixed.

slashCommands.name: new
slashCommands.description: Scaffold code for a new file or project in a workspace
slashCommands.sampleRequest: Create a RESTful API server using typescript

disambiguation.description: The user wants to create a complete Visual Studio Code workspace from scratch, such as a new application or a Visual Studio Code extension. Use this category only if the question relates to generating or creating new workspaces in Visual Studio Code. Do not use this category for updating existing code or generating sample code snippets
examples: Create a sample project which uses the fileSystemProvider API.

slashCommands.name: newNotebook
slashCommands.sampleRequest: How do I create a notebook to load data from a csv file?

disambiguation.description: The user wants to create a new Jupyter notebook in Visual Studio Code.
examples: Create a notebook to analyze this CSV file.

slashCommands.name: setupTests
slashCommands.description: Set up tests in your project (Experimental)
slashCommands.sampleRequest: add playwright tests to my project

disambiguation.description: The user wants to configure project test setup, framework, or test runner. The user does not want to fix their existing tests.
examples: Set up tests for this project.

v.value: I’ll update this Rust project’s dependencies by inspecting the current manifest and lockfile state, then run the standard cargo update flow and verify with tests. I’m starting by reading the manifest to see what can be safely bumped.
I’m gathering exact current dependency versions first so I can update them with minimal risk and then confirm the project still passes tests.

v.generatedTitle: Reviewed Cargo.toml and checked project directory
Reviewed Cargo.toml and checked project directory

v.value: I found a lockfile, so next I’ll refresh dependency resolutions with Cargo and then run the test suite to validate compatibility after the update.

v.value: I identified direct dependencies that are pinned to older major versions, so I’m now updating
to current majors first, then I’ll compile and test to catch any required code fixes.

edits.text:
[package]
name = "model-converter"
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2021"
description = "Safe parser-based PyTorch checkpoint converter to safetensors"

[dependencies]
serde = { version = "1", features = ["derive"] }
serde_json = "1"
serde_yaml = "0.9"
sha2 = "0.11"
zip = { version = "8", default-features = false, features = ["deflate"] }

[dev-dependencies]
safetensors = "0.7"
tempfile = "3"

v.value: The major upgrades are in place, and I hit a compile break from
0.11 output formatting; I’m patching
to hex-encode digests explicitly, then retesting.

Updated dependencies and kept the project green.

What I changed:
1. Bumped direct dependency versions in [Cargo.toml](Cargo.toml):
- sha2: 0.10 -> 0.11
- zip: 2 -> 8
- safetensors (dev-dependency): 0.4 -> 0.7

2. Fixed sha2 0.11 digest formatting changes in:
- [src/iohash.rs](src/iohash.rs)
- [tests/extract_sample.rs](tests/extract_sample.rs)

Validation:
1. Ran dependency resolution update with Cargo.
2. Ran full test suite with cargo test.
3. Result: all tests passed.

If you want, I can also run a stricter check pass next:
1. cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings
2. cargo audit
